purpose: Provide reusable benchmark cases that test whether AOF can convert detected artifact weakness into the correct organizational diagnosis with explicit evidence and fix separation.

cases.name: Wrong Root Cause Rejection
cases.scenario: The artifact is correctly judged weak, but the reviewer attributes the miss to the wrong organizational cause.
expected_fail_if: diagnosis blames a role or policy that is not supported by evidence
the benchmark accepts the wrong cause because the artifact critique itself is reasonable
expected_pass_if: diagnosis ties the weakness to the most supported organizational cause

cases.name: Artifact Fix vs Organization Fix Separation
cases.scenario: The benchmark requires separate artifact-local and organization-level corrective actions.
expected_fail_if: artifact edits are presented as if they alone solve the structural issue
organization changes are omitted when the pattern is recurrent
expected_pass_if: artifact fixes and organization fixes are clearly separated
the benchmark can point to which layer each recommendation belongs to

cases.name: Role Gap vs Loop Gap Classification
cases.scenario: A weak result may come from a missing role, weak role output, or a failed multi-loop correction. The diagnosis must classify which kind of miss occurred.
expected_fail_if: a loop miss is mislabeled as a role miss
a role miss is mislabeled as generic quality weakness
expected_pass_if: the diagnosis distinguishes the dominant miss type
the next corrective action matches that miss type

cases.name: Allocation Blind Spot
cases.scenario: The weak result is caused upstream by a team, council, or resource allocation miss rather than by the visible artifact author alone.
expected_fail_if: allocation or policy causes are ignored
the diagnosis blames execution quality only
expected_pass_if: allocation or policy causes are named when supported
organization recommendations reach the upstream cause
